pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|12=draft|12=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. {{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|12=draft|12=draft}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|12=draft|12=draft}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|12=draft|12=draft}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|12=draft|12=draft}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年|12=draft|12=draft}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年|12=draft|12=draft}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年|12=draft|12=draft}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|12=draft|12=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== External Links == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} --> {{draft categories| [[Category:Short pages second one of the day]] [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:most of these pages appear to have substantial content]] [[Category:春日神社 (関市)]] [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Seki]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kasuga ]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Seki)]] [[ja:春日神社 (関市)]] [[ja:春日神社]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]]

phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|12=draft|12=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|12=draft|12=draft}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|12=draft|12=draft}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|12=draft|12=draft}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|12=draft|12=draft}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年|12=draft|12=draft}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年|12=draft|12=draft}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年|12=draft|12=draft}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|12=draft|12=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== External Links == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} --> {{draft categories| [[Category:Short pages second one of the day]] [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:春日神社 (関市)]] [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Seki]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kasuga ]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Seki)]] [[ja:春日神社 (関市)]] [[ja:春日神社]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]]

phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|12=draft|12=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== External Links == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} --> {{draft categories| [[Category:Short pages second one of the day]] [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:春日神社 (関市)]] [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Seki]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kasuga ]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Seki)]] [[ja:春日神社 (関市)]] [[ja:春日神社]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]]

{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|12=draft|12=draft}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|12=draft|12=draft}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|12=draft|12=draft}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|12=draft|12=draft}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年|12=draft|12=draft}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年|12=draft|12=draft}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年|12=draft|12=draft}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|12=draft|12=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|12=draft|12=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== External Links == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} --> {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Short pages second one of the day]] [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:春日神社 (関市)]] [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Seki]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kasuga ]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Seki)]] [[ja:春日神社 (関市)]] [[ja:春日神社]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]]

{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|12=draft|12=draft}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|12=draft|12=draft}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|12=draft|12=draft}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|12=draft|12=draft}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年|12=draft|12=draft}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年|12=draft|12=draft}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年|12=draft|12=draft}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|12=draft|12=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|12=draft}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|12=draft}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|12=draft}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|12=draft}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|12=draft}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|12=draft}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=draft}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|12=draft}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年|12=draft}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|12=draft}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年|12=draft}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年|12=draft}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年|12=draft}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年|12=draft}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|12=draft}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|12=draft}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest

(side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== External Links == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} --> {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Short pages second one of the day]] [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:春日神社 (関市)]] [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Seki]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kasuga ]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Seki)]] [[ja:春日神社 (関市)]] [[ja:春日神社]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]]

revision.comment: 27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:春日神社 (関市) up to rev 99022306
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=春日神社 |画像=[[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地=〒501-3857：[[岐阜県]][[関市]]南春日町1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}}| |祭神=[[天児屋根命|天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）]]（[[春日大神]]） |社格=[[金幣社]] |創建=[[正応]]元年（[[1288年]]） |本殿= |別名= |例祭=試楽祭（4月第3土曜日）
本楽祭（4月第3日曜日） |神事=童子夜行（どうじやこう） }} '''春日神社'''（かすがじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[関市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[金幣社]]。関鍛冶の守護神。 例祭は[[関まつり]]と呼ばれ、[[古典芸能]]「童子夜行（どうじやこう）」が能舞台で奉納される。また、この例祭に使用される能装束類（63点）は[[昭和]]31年（[[1956年]]）に[[重要文化財]]に指定されており、毎年10月第2日曜日の[[刃物まつり]]などで特別公開されている。 同じく使用される[[能面|能狂言面]]は、[[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）に重要文化財に指定されている。 == 歴史 == [[鎌倉時代]]に大和の国（現在の[[奈良県]]）から何人もの鍛冶刀工が関に移り住んだ。[[正応]]元年（[[1288年]]）その大和鍛冶の出身者である金重と兼永は関鍛冶を総代して奈良の春日大明神を勧請して関鍛冶の守護神とし創建したと云われている。創建時に神宮寺として真言宗新長谷寺の末寺、'''勇猛山大雄寺'''を設けた。 その後、刀工[[孫六兼元]]等の登場により関の刀鍛冶が日本全国に知られることとなり、例祭が盛んになる。 [[永享]]5年（[[1433年]]）初代孫六兼幸が願主となって同地に再建。 [[明治]]時代に至って大雄寺が廃寺となり、鐘楼を遺して建物が撤去された。 明治6年（[[1873年]]）1月[[郷社]]、明治40年（[[1907年]]）神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、昭和29年（[[1954年]]）に金幣社に指定された。 == 能舞台 == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|能舞台]] [[室町時代]]、境内に[[檜]]皮葺きの能舞台を建立。舞台芸能をお祭りにとり入れて、毎年正月、祭事能として神奉能を行うようになる。[[応永]]2年（[[1395年]]）兼吉清治郎が関鍛冶七流関鍛冶七流 - 大和鍛冶の出身者「善定」「奈良」「三阿弥」「徳永」「得印」「良賢」「室屋」の各流派。 を代表して翁役を勤めたのが始まり。 * [[寛文]]5年（[[1665年]]） - 関の領主大島雲八が能舞台を再建。 * 昭和34年（[[1959年]]） - 伊勢湾台風により倒壊。現在の能舞台は従来の建物を再現したものである。 == 神宝殿 == 境内の東北に位置するこの建物は、耐震ならびに耐火を考慮し温度調節機能を備えた文化財収蔵庫である。能装束類などの重要文化財が保管されている。昭和33年（[[1958年]]）竣工。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === ==== 能装束類 ==== いずれも[[室町時代]]末期から[[安土桃山時代]]、16世紀の作品であり、この時代の染織品が1か所にこれだけの数量まとまって伝来するものは稀有であり、日本芸能史・染織史上貴重な資料である。以下の63点が[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。
* 摺箔（すりはく）（桐桜沢瀉（おもだか）文）1領 * 繍箔（ぬいはく）（菊桐山吹文）1領 * 繍箔（草花鳳凰文）1領 * 繍箔（桐松鶴文）1領 * 繍箔（桐楓鴛鴦（おしどり）文）1領 * 繍箔（雪持柳松皮菱文）1領 * 繍箔（雪持柳揚羽蝶文）1領 * 繍箔（松藤揚羽蝶文）2領 * 角帽子（すみぼうし）（菊桐文繍入）1頭 * 角帽子（桐杜若（かきつばた）文繍入）1頭 * 角帽子（杜若文繍入）1頭 * 狩衣（かりぎぬ）（花鳥文銀襴）4領 * 狩衣（花唐草文銀欄）1領 * 狩衣（獣花文銀襴）1領 * 狩衣（雲文緞子）1領 * 法被（はっぴ）（花鳥文銀襴）1領 * 法被（花唐草文銀襴）1領 * 法被（雲竜文黄緞）1領 * 側次（そばつぎ）（花鳥文銀襴）2領 * 側次（花鳥文金襴）1領 * 側次（蓮唐草文緞子）2領 * 直垂（ひたたれ）（鶴亀松竹文）2組 * 直垂（松皮菱松喰鶴文）1組 * 直垂（松喰鶴亀文）1領 * 直垂（路形菊桐松喰鶴文）1領 * 直垂（色紙散松喰鶴文）1領 * 素襖（すおう）（蜻蛉文）1組 * 素襖（宝尽文）1組 * 素襖（草花色紙散文）1領 * 袴（片車輪文）1腰 * 袴（松喰鶴亀松文）1腰 * 袴（立涌桔梗文）1腰 以下は附（つけたり）指定 * 無地熨斗目（むじのしめ）3領 * 長絹（ちょうけん）1領 * 水衣（みずごろも）2領 * 大口 9腰 * 腰帯残欠 4枚 * 尉髪（じょうがみ）、黒頭（くろがしら）、赤頭（あかがしら）、鬘（かずら）各1懸 * 唐櫃（からびつ）1合
==== 木造能狂言面 ==== * 53面（附 木造古楽面8面） === 岐阜県指定重要文化財 === * 刀 銘 兼常{{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14 |url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html |title=刀銘兼常［かたなめいかねつね］ |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2023-10-18}} === 岐阜県指定重要無形民俗文化祭 === * どうじゃこう{{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14 |url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html |title=どうじゃこう |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2023-10-18}} == アクセス == * [[長良川鉄道]][[長良川鉄道越美南線|越美南線]] [[せきてらす前駅]]下車、徒歩で約5分。 * [[東海北陸自動車道]] [[関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|関インターチェンジ]]から車で約5分。 == 周辺 == * [[関鍛冶伝承館]] * [[濃州関所茶屋]] == 参考文献 == * 『解説版新指定重要文化財5 工芸品II』、[[毎日新聞社]]、[[1983年]]（能装束類の解説あり） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ 関春日神社ホームページ] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かすかしんしや せき}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の重要文化財]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:関市の歴史]] [[Category:関市の建築物]] [[Category:春日神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
