{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|12=draft|12=draft}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|12=draft|12=draft}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|12=draft|12=draft}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|12=draft|12=draft}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=draft|12=draft}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|12=draft|12=draft}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年|12=draft|12=draft}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年|12=draft|12=draft}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年|12=draft|12=draft}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|12=draft|12=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest

(traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== External Links == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} --> {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (関市)|version=99022306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Short pages second one of the day]] [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:春日神社 (関市)]] [[Category:Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Seki]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kasuga ]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Seki)]] [[ja:春日神社 (関市)]] [[ja:春日神社]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]]

{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes ==

It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes ==
== External Links == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} --> {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (関市)|version=99022306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Short pages second one of the day]] [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:春日神社 (関市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Seki]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kasuga ]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Seki)]] [[ja:春日神社 (関市)]] [[ja:春日神社]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]]

{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki|qq=}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|qq=}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1288|qq=}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|qq=}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki swordsmithing|qq=}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Festival|qq=}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōji Yakō|qq=}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cutlery Festival|qq=}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noh and Kyōgen masks|qq=}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1288|qq=}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Kanemoto|qq=}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1433|qq=}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Kanemoto|qq=}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1954|qq=}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|qq=}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1395|qq=}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1665|qq=}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1959|qq=}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi–Momoyama period|qq=}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiterasu-mae Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|qq=}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaragawa Railway|qq=}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|qq=}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|qq=}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes ==
== External Links == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} --> {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (関市)|version=99022306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Short pages second one of the day]] [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:春日神社 (関市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Seki]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kasuga ]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Seki)]] [[ja:春日神社 (関市)]] [[ja:春日神社]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]]

{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki|qq=}} Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|de|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|qq=}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1288|qq=}}) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|de|Shintō-Schrein|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Seki|de|Seki (Gifu)|ja|関市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|de|Gifu-Präfektur|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|qq=}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki swordsmithing|qq=}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Festival|qq=}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōji Yakō|qq=}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cutlery Festival|qq=}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noh and Kyōgen masks|qq=}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the {{ill|Kamakura period|de|Kamakura-Zeit|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ({{ill|1288|de|1288|ja|1288年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1288|qq=}}), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Kanemoto|qq=}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ({{ill|1433|de|1433|ja|1433年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1433|qq=}}), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Kanemoto|qq=}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ({{ill|1873|de|1873|ja|1873年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}}), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ({{ill|1907|de|1907|ja|1907年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ({{ill|1954|de|1954|ja|1954年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1954|qq=}}), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|qq=}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ({{ill|1395|de|1395|ja|1395年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1395|qq=}}), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ({{ill|1665|de|1665|ja|1665年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1665|qq=}}): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ({{ill|1959|de|1959|ja|1959年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1959|qq=}}): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ({{ill|1958|de|1958|ja|1958年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1958|qq=}}). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late {{ill|Muromachi period|de|Muromachi-Zeit|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} to the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|de|Azuchi-Momoyama-Zeit|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuchi–Momoyama period|qq=}}, dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiterasu-mae Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|qq=}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaragawa Railway|qq=}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|qq=}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|qq=}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes ==
== External Links == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} --> {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (関市)|version=99022306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Short pages second one of the day]] [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:春日神社 (関市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Seki]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kasuga ]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Seki)]] [[ja:春日神社 (関市)]] [[ja:春日神社]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]]

pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiterasu-mae Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|de|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|ja|長良川鉄道越美南線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line|qq=}}, operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaragawa Railway|qq=}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|de|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|ja|東海北陸自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|qq=}}. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|qq=}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) |画像 = [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 01.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地 = 〒501-3857：[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]][[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki]] Minami-Kasuga-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|28|56.42|N|136|55|0.76|E|region:JP-21_scale:20000|name=春日神社}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|de|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto (amenokoyane no mikoto)|lt_ja=天児屋根命（あめのこやねのみこと）|lt_de=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ({{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}}) |社格 = {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|qq=}} |創建 = Shōō 1 ([[:en:1288|1288]]) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = Shigorakusai (3rd Saturday of April)
Hongakusai (3rd Sunday of April) |神事 = Dōji Yakō (童子夜行) }} '''Kasuga Shrine''' (春日神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. == Overview == {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|qq=}}. It enshrines the guardian deity of {{ill|Seki swordsmithing|de|Seki swordsmithing|ja|関鍛冶|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki swordsmithing|qq=}}. Its annual festival is called the {{ill|Seki Festival|de|Seki-Fest|ja|関まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Festival|qq=}}, during which the classical performing art {{ill|Dōji Yakō|de|Dōji Yakō|ja|童子夜行|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōji Yakō|qq=}} is offered on the Noh stage. The Noh costumes (63 items) used in this festival were designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in Shōwa 31 (1956). They are specially exhibited every year on the second Sunday of October during the {{ill|Cutlery Festival|de|Cutlery Festival|ja|刃物まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cutlery Festival|qq=}}, among other occasions. The {{ill|Noh and Kyōgen masks|de|Noh- und Kyōgen-Masken|ja|能面|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noh and Kyōgen masks|qq=}} used similarly were designated as Important Cultural Properties in Heisei 22 (2010). == History == During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], many swordsmiths from Yamato Province (present-day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) moved to Seki. In Shōō 1 ([[:en:1288|1288]]), two swordsmiths of Yamato origin, Kaneshige and Kanenaga, are said to have led all the Seki smiths in enshrining {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|de|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}} here as the guardian deity of Seki swordsmithing. At the time of its foundation, a temple was established as its jingū-ji under the Shingon sect’s Shin-Hasedera: '''Yūmōzan Daiōji''' (勇猛山大雄寺). Later, with the emergence of swordsmiths such as {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼元|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Kanemoto|qq=}}, the fame of the Seki swordsmiths spread throughout Japan, and the annual festival flourished. In Eikyō 5 ([[:en:1433|1433]]), it was rebuilt on the same site under the sponsorship of the first-generation {{ill|Magoroku Kanemoto|de|Magoroku Kanemoto|ja|孫六兼幸|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magoroku Kanemoto|qq=}} (also known as Kaneyuki) as the main contributor. By the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, Daiōji temple was abolished, leaving only its belltower. - Meiji 6 ([[:en:1873|1873]]), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|de|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} (village shrine). - Meiji 40 ([[:en:1907|1907]]), it was specified for official offerings (shinsen heihakukyōshin). - Shōwa 29 ([[:en:1954|1954]]), it was designated a {{ill|Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|de|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha (shrine Rank)|qq=}}. == Noh stage == [[Image:Kasuga Shrine 03.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Noh stage]] In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], a Noh stage with a cypress-bark roof was constructed within the shrine precincts. They incorporated performing arts into the annual festival, conducting a dedication performance each New Year called Shinponō (sacred Noh). In Ōei 2 ([[:en:1395|1395]]), Kaneyoshi Seijirō, representing one of the Seven Schools of Seki swordsmithingSeven Schools of Seki Swordsmithing: Founded by smiths who came from Yamato, including “Zentei,” “Nara,” “Sanami,” “Tokunaga,” “Tokuin,” “Ryōken,” and “Muroya.”, performed as the Okina role to begin this tradition. * Kanbun 5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]): The local lord Ōshima Kumppachi rebuilt the Noh stage. * Shōwa 34 ([[:en:1959|1959]]): The stage collapsed due to the Isewan Typhoon. The current Noh stage is a reconstruction of the original building. == Shinpo-den == Located to the northeast of the main grounds, this building is a cultural properties storage constructed with earthquake- and fire-resistance in mind, featuring climate controls. Important Cultural Properties such as the Noh costumes are preserved here. It was completed in Shōwa 33 ([[:en:1958|1958]]). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === ==== Noh Costumes ==== All are works from the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] to the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]], dating to the 16th century. It is rare that such a large number of textiles from this era survive in one place; they are valuable materials in the history of Japanese performing arts and textile arts. The following 63 items have been designated as Important Cultural Properties:
* Surihaku (stenciled foil) (Paulownia, Cherry, and Water Plantain motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (embroidery and foil) (Chrysanthemum, Paulownia, and Yamabuki flower motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (floral and phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (pine, wisteria, and swallowtail butterfly motif) – 2 robes * Sumibōshi (corner-cap headpiece) (Chrysanthemum and Paulownia motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Paulownia and Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Sumibōshi (Iris motif with embroidery) – 1 piece * Kariginu (hunting robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 4 robes * Kariginu (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (beast-flower pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Kariginu (cloud pattern in damask) – 1 robe * Happi (short robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (arabesque pattern in silver brocade) – 1 robe * Happi (cloud-dragon pattern in yellow brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (side-seamed robe) (floral-bird pattern in silver brocade) – 2 robes * Sobatsugi (floral-bird pattern in gold brocade) – 1 robe * Sobatsugi (lotus arabesque pattern in damask) – 2 robes * Hitatare (traditional men’s outfit) (crane and turtle with pine and bamboo motif) – 2 sets * Hitatare (Matsukawabishi and pine-crane motif) – 1 set * Hitatare (pine, crane, and turtle motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (roadway-shaped pattern with chrysanthemum, paulownia, and pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Hitatare (scattered shikishi squares with pine-crane motif) – 1 robe * Suō (plain outer robe) (dragonfly motif) – 1 set * Suō (treasure motif) – 1 set * Suō (grass and scattered shikishi pattern) – 1 robe * Hakama (kata-guruma motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (pine-crane-turtle-pine motif) – 1 pair * Hakama (vertical wavy stripe and bellflower motif) – 1 pair The following are attached designations: * Three muji noshime (plain lined robes) * Chōken (long Noh robe) – 1 robe * Mizu-goromo (water robe) – 2 robes * Ōkuchi (large trousers) – 9 pairs * Waistband remnants – 4 pieces * Wig set: jyōgami, kuro-gashira, aka-gashira, kazura – 1 each * Karabitsu (Chinese-style chest) – 1 chest
==== Wooden Noh and Kyōgen Masks ==== * 53 masks (附: 8 ancient musical masks in wood) === Gifu Prefectural Important Cultural Property === * Katana (inscribed “Kanetsune”){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12481.html|title=刀銘兼常 [Katana mei Kanetsune]|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} === Gifu Prefectural Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Dōja kō (どうじゃこう){{Cite web|和書|date=2015-10-14|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7956.html|title=どうじゃこう|publisher=岐阜県|accessdate=2023-10-18}} == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Sekiterasu-mae Station|de|Sekiterasu-mae Station|ja|せきてらす前駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekiterasu-mae Station|qq=}} on the [[:en:Etsumi-Nan Line|Nagaragawa Railway Etsumi-Nan Line]], operated by the {{ill|Nagaragawa Railway|de|Nagaragawa Railway|ja|長良川鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaragawa Railway|qq=}}. * About 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|de|Seki Interchange|ja|関インターチェンジ (岐阜県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Interchange (Gifu Prefecture)|qq=}} on the [[:en:Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway]]. == Nearby == * {{ill|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|de|Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|ja|関鍛冶伝承館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki Swordsmithing Tradition Museum|qq=}} * {{ill|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|de|Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|ja|濃州関所茶屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōshū Sekisho Chaya|qq=}} == References == * ‘‘Kaisetsu-ban Shin Shitei Jūyō Bunkazai 5 Kōgeihin II’’ (Commentary on Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 5: Craftworks II), Mainichi Shinbunsha, 1983. (Contains commentary on the Noh costumes.) == Notes ==
== External Links == * [http://www.seki-kasuga.com/ Kasuga Shrine Official Website] {{commonscat|Kasuga-jinja (Seki)}} --> {{translated page|ja|春日神社 (関市)|version=99022306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Short pages second one of the day]] [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:short pages as of 03-26-2025 hopefully the last batch of short pages with any ones that lack content]] [[Category:春日神社 (関市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Seki]] [[Category:Buildings in Seki]] [[Category:Kasuga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kasuga ]] [[sn:Kasuga Shrine (Seki)]] [[ja:春日神社 (関市)]] [[ja:春日神社]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein]]

phoenix motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Pine, and Crane motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (Paulownia, Maple, and Mandarin Duck motif) – 1 robe * Nuihaku (snow-laden willow and Sagamibishi motif) – 1
